title: Net-New Story Gap Review — 2026-05-15 (HRREC-82977)
content:
TL;DR — five story fixes to prioritise

  • HRREC-91999 — Recruiter sees blue "unread marker" removed after clicking in the vie...
    Read/unread behaviour affects handoffs when more than one recruiter touches the same candidate. Pick the product rule (per person vs shared), write it in plain language in Jira, then align dependent stories.
  • HRREC-92022 — My Conversations will require an XO (small) refactor
    The ticket still reads too thin to size or test with confidence. Add clear recruiter-facing scenarios and acceptance before committing build time.
  • HRREC-92011 — Privacy Admin purges PDTs for 2-way email - Candidate/Job App
    Deleting candidate communications touches trust, compliance, and what recruiters still see in the UI. Pair each purge-related promise in Jira with a privacy-admin walkthrough and recruiter-visible empty states.
  • HRREC-91997 — Recruiter sees list of messages with unread ones marked in blue
    Read/unread behaviour affects handoffs when more than one recruiter touches the same candidate. Pick the product rule (per person vs shared), write it in plain language in Jira, then align dependent stories.
  • HRREC-92035 — Email communications are visible in Activity Stream, Timeline and Can...
    If reporting or timelines are out of scope or partial, teams discover gaps only late in UAT. List which recruiter-visible history is in or out of scope for this epic, and where gaps are accepted risk.

Executive summary (for PM)

  • For most customers, two-way recruiting email still behaves like “send from Workday, read replies in the mailbox”—say that plainly in launch and training so expectations match reality.
  • Purging or deleting candidate communications is powerful: only promise “we keep a history” when privacy admins confirm what can be removed and what still appears to recruiters.
  • Tenant-level recruiting message templates can override what a recruiter drafted—decide how you prevent silent blank sends and how you want customers to configure templates.
  • Unread counts, bells, and the conversational inbox need one clear story for who sees what when a candidate replies—otherwise recruiters get conflicting cues across tickets.

Epic-level notes

  • Many tickets repeat the same “make the server match the screen” check—use the first “REST parity spot-check” block in the table as a reusable pattern, then copy the findings into sibling stories instead of re-proving the same risk on every row.
  • Compose, send-with-files, validations, and error-handling slices should share one agreed happy path and failure path so QA does not get conflicting mocks.
  • Unread and read markers need a product decision before notification and badge work (for example 91997–91999 with 91975/91946) is treated as done.
  • Agency vs non-agency tabs and compose (91991, 92007, 92008, 92029) share one predicate decision—one short architecture review lowers duplicate defects.
Story Gap score (%) PM lens QA lens XO lens Verdict Suggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)

HRREC-91946
[Initialisation] Recruiter can see Email Task on SSP (XO Only)

30% — limited tension in this heuristic pass; lenses largely agree
  • Jira — Make sure what happens on the server matches what recruiters see in the app for this story (errors, limits, and send behaviour).
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) If the ticket mentions system hooks, plan a short engineering check-in—search hits rarely prove end-to-end wiring.
  • Deployment Agent — (Replay placeholder—not live Deployment Agent answers.) In a live review, ask DA which admin toggles mirror common customer misconfiguration for this area.
  • Jira — Add one negative path tied to this summary.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, search Salomon on this key’s words—not a generic inbox checklist.

XO MCP — (Replay placeholder—condensed from stub XO text.) XO read-only pass surfaced: Recruiting Email PRU/PU (Candidate Email create, External/Internal candidate parms), WATS Recruiting Email variable containers; Conversation Message (+ Derived), Status, Sender; Notification / Notification Configuration... Risk — Risk from absence: generic Notification classes do not prove inbound reply event routing or conversational-email-specific notif types; SUV + REST contract still required per story.
  • Finding: No strong cross-lens conflict showed up in this heuristic replay.
  • Recommended next step: Re-read Jira for “Email Task is visible” on HRREC-91946; if nothing new surfaces, keep scenarios as written.

Beyond Jira — Email Task is visible
Given A recruiter with Modify/Send on conversational email where this slice applies on a tenant staged for this epic, with the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel (SSP); Jira’s first *Given* alrea...
When I exercise the recruiter-visible interaction described by: Recruiter can see Email Task on SSP (XO Only)
Then Observable UI, notification, or persisted thread state matches the acceptance criteria for this slice in recruiter-verifiable terms (harden wording under 430 / 319 before sign-off)

REST parity spot-check (epic-wide template—use once per review)
Given I stage the tenant and candidate context this ticket’s REST mentions
When I exercise the recruiter action and capture network responses for the operations Jira implies
Then UI errors, persisted thread state, and REST payloads stay aligned for this slice; reuse this pattern on sibling REST-mentioned stories

Replay note: Jira scenarios look ample and heuristic tension is low—no extra compact BDD blocks beyond the first scenario extension.

HRREC-91974
PH: Recruiter sees Empty state of email task if opened

31% — agency matrix called out
  • Jira — agency matrix — align with security domains and tab visibility siblings.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) If first-use / empty-thread UX is in scope, verify copy and actions match recruiter expectations on a cold start.
  • Deployment Agent — (Replay placeholder—not live Deployment Agent answers.) In a live review, ask DA which admin toggles mirror common customer misconfiguration for this area.
  • Jira — Add one negative path tied to this summary.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, search Salomon on this key’s words—not a generic inbox checklist.

XO MCP — (Replay placeholder—condensed from stub XO text.) Same catalog snapshot as the row above (one epic-wide search in this replay). For HRREC-91974, confirm which layout / REST fields drive first-open empty state vs first-message thread chrome. Risk — Same catalog-level risk as rows above—confirm wiring for this key.
  • Finding: No strong cross-lens conflict showed up in this heuristic replay.
  • Recommended next step: Re-read Jira for “Opening Email task initially shows the ...” on HRREC-91974; if nothing new surfaces, keep scenarios as written.

Beyond Jira — Opening Email task initially shows the empty state for new conversations
Given A recruiter with Modify/Send on conversational email where this slice applies on a tenant staged for this epic; Jira’s first *Given* already includes: I am a recruiter with access to the 2-way emai...
When I open the 2-way email task in the SSP when no conversation exists yet for the candidate states listed in Jira
Then Empty-state copy and affordances match Jira for each listed combination (no phantom thread or misleading send entry)

Agency vs non-agency predicates
Given the candidate has agency-linked and direct applications (or agency association toggles) as exercised in QA staging
When I open the email task and any tabs or compose entry points this story owns
Then chrome and permissions follow the portfolio rules for each case without thread bleed or misleading empty tabs

HRREC-91975
PH: Recruiter receives a notification

40% — agency matrix called out
  • Jira — agency matrix — align with security domains and tab visibility siblings.; notifications — align bell vs My Conversations entry and domain access.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) If internal search returns hits, check whether bell-style alerts vs the conversational inbox could confuse recruiters for this path.
  • Deployment Agent — (Replay placeholder—not live Deployment Agent answers.) In a live review, ask DA how Reply-To and notification routing should be staged for realistic recruiter habits.
  • Jira — Check who gets notified vs Reply-To habits for this flow.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, search Salomon on this key’s words—not a generic inbox checklist.

XO MCP — (Replay placeholder—condensed from stub XO text.) Same catalog snapshot as the row above (one epic-wide search in this replay). For HRREC-91975, map framework notification types and conversational notification domain access to the inbound-reply path this summary describes—not just generic Notific... Risk — Because generic notification parts exist in the catalog, still confirm inbound-reply behaviour specifically for this email story.
  • Finding: For “Recruiter receives a notification”, the three lenses mostly line up; biggest residual note: agency matrix called out.
  • Recommended next step: Tighten Jira with two recruiter-visible checks for “Recruiter receives a notification”.

Beyond Jira — Inbound reply triggers framework notification for participants
Given A recruiter with Modify/Send on conversational email where this slice applies on a tenant staged for this epic, with the 2-way email task and thread chrome described in Jira; Jira’s first *Given* a...
When An inbound reply event occurs that should drive conversational notifications per Jira for my user’s domain access
Then Notifications and deep links respect conversational notification domain access — actionable only when policy allows

Agency vs non-agency predicates
Given the candidate has agency-linked and direct applications (or agency association toggles) as exercised in QA staging
When I open the email task and any tabs or compose entry points this story owns
Then chrome and permissions follow the portfolio rules for each case without thread bleed or misleading empty tabs

Inbound routing vs My Conversations (SMS legacy)
Given inbound reply events exist for my tenant and domain access varies between recruiters
When a candidate reply lands while I do or do not hold conversational notification domain access
Then I only receive actionable notifications and deep links when policy allows, and land on the correct candidate email thread

HRREC-91978
PH: Admin can enable or disable 2-way email as a comms channel

38% — minor residual signals—confirm in a live evidence pass
  • Jira — Review BDD Scenarios vs compose/send/notification siblings for overlapping REST contract.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) Run Salomon on this ticket’s exact wording in a live review; this replay only mirrors summary/gap keywords—not real hits.
  • Deployment Agent — (Replay placeholder—not live Deployment Agent answers.) In a live review, ask DA which admin toggles mirror common customer misconfiguration for this area.
  • Jira — Add one negative path tied to this summary.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, search Salomon on this key’s words—not a generic inbox checklist.

XO MCP — (Replay placeholder—condensed from stub XO text.) Same catalog snapshot as the row above (one epic-wide search in this replay). For HRREC-91978, tie tenant admin toggles to Recruiting Email / task enablement implied in Jira before UI copy locks. Risk — Risk from absence: generic Notification classes do not prove inbound reply event routing or conversational-email-specific notif types; SUV + REST contract still required per story.
  • Finding: For “Admin can enable or disable 2-way email a...”, the three lenses mostly line up; biggest residual note: minor residual signals—confirm in a live evidence pass.
  • Recommended next step: Tighten Jira with two recruiter-visible checks for “Admin can enable or disable 2-way email as a ...”.

Beyond Jira — Enable channel for pilot tenant
Given A tenant administrator on a tenant staged for this epic, with the 2-way email task and thread chrome described in Jira; Jira’s first *Given* already includes: I am a tenant admin
When I toggle the two-way recruiting email communications channel in the admin surfaces Jira describes and re-open recruiter-facing email task chrome affected by that change
Then Recruiter-visible channel availability, messaging, and email task entry points match Jira for both enabled and disabled states—no orphaned tasks or stale chrome

Tenant admin channel ripple
Given a tenant admin toggles the 2-way recruiting email channel the story names
When a recruiter opens the candidate email task on the same tenant before and after the toggle
Then recruiter-visible availability, copy, and errors match Jira for both on and off states

HRREC-91979
PH: Recruiter does not have access to the compose email task

32% — limited tension in this heuristic pass; lenses largely agree
  • Jira — Make sure what happens on the server matches what recruiters see in the app for this story (errors, limits, and send behaviour).
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) If the ticket mentions system hooks, plan a short engineering check-in—search hits rarely prove end-to-end wiring.

  • Jira — Add one negative path tied to this summary.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, search Salomon on this key’s words—not a generic inbox checklist.

XO MCP — (Replay placeholder—condensed from stub XO text.) Same catalog snapshot as the row above (one epic-wide search in this replay). For HRREC-91979, prove domain-level View-without-Send gating in XO matches hidden compose affordances in the UI slice.   • Finding: No strong cross-lens conflict showed up in this heuristic replay.
  • Recommended next step: Re-read Jira for “Add button is hidden for read-only recr...” on HRREC-91979; if nothing new surfaces, keep scenarios as written.

Beyond Jira — Add button is hidden for read-only recruiters
Given A recruiter with Modify/Send on conversational email where this slice applies on a tenant staged for this epic; Jira’s first *Given* already includes: my security role grants me "View" access but n...
When I open the 2-way email task, thread list, and every Add / Compose affordance named in Jira while my domain access stays View-only per the first scenario
Then Observable UI, notification, or persisted thread state matches the acceptance criteria for this slice in recruiter-verifiable terms (harden wording under 430 / 319 before sign-off)

View-only security on compose affordances
Given my security role matches the View / no-Send cases in Jira for this domain
When I open the 2-way email task, thread list, and any Add / Compose entry points this slice exposes
Then send and compose entry points stay hidden or blocked with recruiter-clear copy—no back-door send
